column35: Struggled to pick heroes. The game was a slog. I just felt so disheartened by the setup I didn’t want to play again. Looking back on it She hulk might have been a better choice of a hero.
It was total slog, got one epic turn out of Scars rare, but it was way too late
The villain setup was unique, but I don't care for Skaar and King BP, which made choosing and recruiting Heroes seem more like a chore than it usually is.

We tried something different this time, 1 player focusing on 3-cost cards and the other player pretty much everything else. This made KOing with Death's Head very difficult for one of us, but we managed the city well and got no escape. Psy-Lord was useless as usual in our games, I still can't find a way to make him work reliably (or at all for that matter). We got Miek rare and could use it transformed once and it really helped.

Was trying to bring in more recruit for shattering and focusing. Sadly didn't succeed with Psi-Lord's focus. Strong Guy went off the second game but still wasn't super fast but a win against Exodus is a win. Maybe if I hadn't drawn 2 masterstrikes in the first 2 turns and had to spend so much on KOing them.

Had a tough 2 games. Decided to go for okoye for wound manipulation and white wolf to help weaken villains. Ended up taking a long time due to having to skip a turn multiple times to put wounds back. Game 2 felt better but an extremely I'll timed Mastermind Tactic ruined a potential win turn, then it took 5 more turns later.

Skaar was awesome. I got his rare in both games and was able to use Pharaoh to put him out of time for 2 turns back to back. Really like this setup as it was different. The game went on way too long though with only 3 cards left in the deck. I think FFU could have been replaced with General Okoye for better effect.

column35:
Well, that was unexpectedly fast! My wife picked up lots of recruit, which gave her Silver Surfer's rare, which gave her Thing's rare. She then fought everything! Key was getting Endless Hunger to eat 5 of her starters...
I meanwhile had barely gotten going. I'd made a decent Scarlet Spider/Mockingbird deck and had just hit Shield Level 2, but still, can't complain!
Range heroes went the distance. Submitted the second attempt with fewer turns but first attempt was more fun to play. Carnage is always a worthy opponent.

Tried to build a Ranged/Covert deck in one hand, and a Instinct/Tech in the other. Ranged side(snash108) was successful! Instinct side(SBPKECN) treaded water. 

Carnage went hungry with all but 1 of his tactics, as the deck was empty! At one point,this happened, and I had enough recruit after all my attacking was done, so I bought another Iceman card, paid 2 recruit 2 draw a card with Silver Surfer, drew the Iceman card and hit Carnage again. 

Absolutely ridiculous!!
Pretty fun. We got to the realization that we should get our decks small enough to become unfeastable but still got mastermind every turn or any villains attempting to escape.

Ice man does ice man things but actually it was bishop that really carried the blue, iceman was there to enable the bishop uncommon.  I got all 3 of them in my deck! The other deck was able to pick up mockingbird rare but it only came in on the 2nd last turn.  It was interesting we were trying to figure out if we wanted an extra turn to kill more things, and we ended up taking the extra turn but I hope it doesn't come around to bite us in the butt!
S.H.I.E.L.D. has been a while, I liked that. Not sure if it will be fast enough, the S.H.I.E.L.D. level took a while to build up. MVP was Silver Surfers 'The Power Cosmic'.

So many KOs to be had! WaspWoman cleaned up here with a solid Bishop/Iceman deck build, that thinned quickly and ended up with Bishop, Iceman, and Nightcrawler's rares. She was extremely worried about feasts, but she got her deck down to 6 cards (three of them rares!), and feasting was no longer a concern.
First attempt, I forgot to recruit heroes correctly for Chivalrous Duel.  Had to take another crack at it.  Second attempt was much better.

The chess game was an artful master piece on Morgan Le Fay and the villainous side of Electro/Green Goblin. I knew with me getting close to a potential top 16 I had to pull out all the stops here and try and line up the combo of discard for this villains game. 

Game one was a check-mate for Morgan as I was able to unlock the combo but was a bit late in doing so as I only had one Electro uncommon to try and go off every turn and ended on 33 turns.

Turn 2 however, I was able to greedily steal the Green Goblin rare early and two Green Goblin make another Hydra unit have dodge with 2 green goblin kidnap a bystander if you discard a card to really get the engine purring. I could hear the Goblin screaming happily as I thinned my deck out with Electro's common enough to cycle through my deck ever turn that netted me +2 Bystanders with a Morgan hit.

This allowed me to only need to buy Madam Hydras plus any other dodge and a Black Knight Rare to finish the job in fantastic fashion and ten turns earlier than my previous fight. Turn 23 with a massive VP score on one side in the hopes it will be enough. 

Will the Green Goblin's laugh get me the win and top 16? Tune in to find out more on this exciting episode of Amazing Spider-Man... HAHAHHAHAHAHAHAH!!
Hulkling was brought primarily for his uncommon. Brought wasp for extra covert and card draw. Hulking was on fire on one side, while Wiccan shredded on the other!

The heroes provided kind of sucked and didn't support my choices that well. Which may be my fault for choosing the wrong ones then. However, Chivalrous Duel and getting Thor down as soon as possible didn't prove easy. My first attempt felt rather slow, but I managed to not let anything escape. Getting the 7 attack from the same hero source for Morgan Le Fay turned out to be challenging. Like I said, the heroes provided didn't do great at that either. Wiccan had cards that could give you 3 attack per card, but then you would need 3 of them. His other common provided recruit and that didn't help, unless you had 3 of them with an Irresistible bribe. Black Knight actually helped my first game, but his recruit common is one of the worst cards in the game (imo), at least for this setup, his other common requires a mono-coloured HQ (which happened in HQ), combined with his 6-cost or another of his Empower commons.

My choices were made with the hope for a lot of additional bystanders rescued, which didin't turn out that great. In game 1 I did get the Ultron uncommon for that in HQ from the start (the other 2 didn't show up), in the second game he only turned up near the end and only kdinapped/rescued 4 bystanders. In game 1 he didn't do much better, as I ended up drawing him without many triggers most turns I did draw him. What did help though, was that Ant-Man and Ultron (which both went into deck 1 in game 1 and into deck 2 in game 2) could defeat Morgan Le Fay if you got a little lucky (Ultron's 2-cost required his uncommon and 4 other Tech cards (counting the uncommon), or another copy of itself and 3 other tech cards. Ant-Man's cards luckily had lots of draw and the uncommon benefited from that draw as well. Also, not having recruit in that hero deck helped a lot to get to 7 for Morgan. Deck 2 for Game 1 (Deck1 for game 2) got the Enchantress' cards, Soul Sacrifice and Irresistible Bribe, which helped KO and defeat Thor or other problems in the city. The Ultron and Ant-Man deck could almost not KO anything and also usually not prevent the Master Strike effect (because it didn't get many/any covert cards).

Managing the City felt like a bit of a puzzle. The Master of Evil (WW II), came too soon to really benefit from their savior bonuses/prevent their penalties, so I just let them hang out in the city until I didn't get penalties or just didn't get any benefits. Asgardian Warriors are meh Henchmen, they didn't help very much, but at least they didn't actively make the game harder either. KO-ing proved to be a problem for both Decks. In game 1 took the chance of Thor overrunning by choosing to defeat Morgan Le Fay instead (as I finally had 7 attack from a single source). Thor did in fact overrun by Plot Twist as well the next turn, but it was only Asgard's second Triumph. I think if I hadn't done that, game 1 would probably also have lasted many turns longer.

Since we got a lot of Bystanders in our first game without really trying, we decided to play into it in our second game by bringing Steve Rogers and Captain 1941. Both sides did decently, with the major hurdle for us being getting through Morgan's Chivalrous Duel. Eventually my deck was able to get Enchantress' bribery and recruit game going, and my partner was getting enough attack via the two Steves to start whittling her down. We had fun with this setup, thanks!
Thor just kepy coming and coming. I enjoyed this set up. The chivalrous duel made me pay attention to recruiting only certain characters for fighting. It was different but enjoyable.

column35: Wish I had time for a round 2. Duel was difficult
Both games ended up with the exact same VP score, but 1 took 1 less turn and 1 less escape

Had fun theory crafting. Unfortunately in practice not everything turned out great, been quite unlucky and had escaping villains in early game. (Besides Thor). 
Combos that worked out:
1st game: enchantress 6cost + 3cost, with KOing a card of at least 3 cost, and having extra red (from wasp mostly).
1st game: amulet of avalon. Cloack and dagger really helped in keeping the HQ mostly red and/or blue. So two of tlblack knights common could also hit Morgana. 
2nd game: could buy black knight rare, which after having Thor could one hit Morgana.
Combo looking good in theory:
Speedballs common could be quite easily triggered, and would be a cheap option for getting 4 attack out of the cards. But only two of them appeared in the HQ/lair, so the only time it worked out was when I drew arount 8 cards in one turn.

Chivalrous duel is still super annoying. Having rounda of lik 12-15 attack midgane with an empty cit, but no chance to hit mastermind is a real pain. On one hand you have to keep deck thin, but its hard to do when you have to keep the HQ going, searching for combo cards. 

Also: Namorita was useful against Thor. May check out the Speedball- Cloak and Dagger combo as a third option just for fun.

Too bad for the early overrun in both games... (2nd game was 5 turns longer, with almost same VP/turn).

column35: This was actually a great puzzle to solve! Eventually, Black Knight and Speedball were built up enough in the left hand to take out Morgana every few turns. Right hand was there any time Thor showed up to keep him from getting a final Triumph of Asgard. It really came down to the wire though!

Really enjoyed the setup, the only thing stopping it getting a 5 was the scheme being able to cause escapes just due to the shuffle, with no way to react (except hellcats rare happening to ve in your hand.)
It was a tough decision for hero choices as my original thought was to go with double wolverine but Photon made me consider discard synergy instead and went with Angel. Really glad I did this as they worked together really well with a sprinkling of domino's cards. They did the heavy lifting while wolverine and he'll cat built up both sides generating over 20 attack onthe final few turns.

Paisley had a blast Ice Manning the enemies to death. I supported by buying anything not blue or green. Also, we weren't sure if locations were villains or other, so we put them under villains. There was one location in the villain deck at the end, in case we were wrong.

Well, my 2nd attempt did a good job of showing me just how lucky my 1st attempt was! 2nd was a quick loss, but first was great: not once did I draw Goons followed by a Twist. The Goons were also spaced out so I could defeat them for KOs without letting anything escape. Once they were in my Victory Pile, I KO’d them with Blazing Hellfire. Between Hell on Wheels and Focus Chi I had a lot of Recruit and one deck picked up Living Weapon along with more rares later. I even found Ghost Rider’s rare useful! Also, the Destroyer was one of the first Villains to appear so I was able to take out and KO a bunch of Shield cards. Great game, I could have actually finished sooner but there were so many points sitting in the city and my decks were going so well that I just had to grab some!
Our first game Iceman was clutch on killing stuff and hell cat did her job of sending Maggia goons to the bottom or to our vp before they could come out
Super tough. The special rule should've been extended to turn 5/6 for this one. Also, Photon is like my least favorite hero lol. I hate the hyper speed mechanic!

column35: Organized Crime Wave was a tricky scheme but I definitely enjoyed it. I made sure to have plenty of Ranged cards and very few tech cards to avoid most of the fight effects from the villain groups. I've never been so wary of the Maggia Goons before, they really can swarm and take over the city in a single villain phase so I had to be careful to manage the city even more deliberately than usual. This was all complicated by the locations which made it undesirable to fight in certain areas but ai had to do so anyway because the Maggia would not stay in the victory piles. The Grim Reaper was also tricky since you had to fight him once as a mastermind, once as a location and fight locations whenever possible to lower his strength

Whew. I am glad to be through that one. So much going on in the city, I was doing everything i could to prevent escapes while trying to tackle the mastermind. It felt like a struggle from the very first round all the way to the end.
This scheme is already rough and the added locations didn't make it any easier. Came down to the wire both games. Fortunately got lucky with my scheme twists.

At first I tried to use tech heroes together like Iron Man and Darkhawk with Domino, but I got overwhelmed by early master strikes making villains too strong and getting tons of wounds. Total disaster! Round two I switched to using Wolverine, Quicksilver and Hellcat to draw through the deck and that made all the difference--as did getting Wolverine and Monica's rares. The other deck mostly used the strength Quicksilver cards to hyperspeed after thinning the deck. It was down to the wire but I was generating so much attack by the end I could hit Grim Reaper even with some locations in play.

Fingers crossed they make the cut, but I think it'll be number 16 if they make it...

The decks did what I thought they would, though in the first game cards ideal for one strategy would end up in the deck running the other, lest they feed the Scheme.  That first game was quicker but had several escapes.  Second was more in control, but I got VP greedy and took too long; a win, but not likely good enough to rate well in this venue.

Pretty much the haymaker throwing matchup I expected.  Realizing late game that I was safe from losing via scheme twist unless the city went to hell took a lot of pressure off and made the game really fun.

Deceptively tough setup. We chose Logan and Carnage hoping to get some early KO's from their kit......neither KO cards appeared either game. Oh well. Played way more Gwenpool than I ever had previously. Her Covert common kinda kicks some butt and her bystander rescue card came in clutch for covering up the Ghost Racers. Got really lucky and pulled Evil DPs Tactics in just the right order every time. Anyway, it was a fun little setup that made us strategize consistently.
Fortunate enough to have no strikes come out before I could finish deadpool with carnage’s combo.. albeit being a one sided hand.
Great setup - Deadpool is always a good time

This was such a tough one! The first game ALMOST went silky smooth: I had 2 tactics by turn 20 and was keeping the city empty. Then I hit the "Shuffle this back in" tactic FOUR TIMES and just couldn't do it. Proxima Midnight didn't provide the superpower activations I was expecting to--Gwenpool did plenty by herself--so I switched over to Carnage in G2. 

Even in losing, Old man Logan was an MVP! Between the two games I KO'd more than a combined 35 wounds!

Had Evil Deadpool ressurrect THREE TIMES. Unfortunately never cleared that tactic and was unable to finish the job. Some early rares did me good in my first attempt, but not good enough sadly. The sheer amount of wounds wasn't enough for Deadpool and Ms. Marvel to keep up, especially at twist 5. Luckily I ended up with a decent chunk of VP for a loss, and the last scheme twist happened with only one other card in the VD so I got to up my turn count quite a bit lol. I thought about waiting for after 8pm to play but didn't have time to wait sadly. Probably woulnd't have made the difference but overall, a nice enough setup. Another tournament in the books! gg

This one was a blast. Even though we lost, Deadpool was fun and funny. We actually hadn't played this set yet. He is such a jerk haha. We enjoyed all the heros. The scheme is what got us. Thank you for allowing us in this competition even though we didn't do well we had a great time!.

I struggle when playing Timer schemes where you just lose when the last one is drawn.  It's fine, but not my favorite.  Both decks were doing well and got a nice big attack from deck 1 of 25, good for two hits on Evil Deadpool but of course one was the "if not final tactic" so wasn't able to get as much progress as I'd hoped.  Tough match to win, needs a little luck to help.

I know the thematic of the game was funny but the game was not. We basically were trying to clean wounds from the deck and when we were just one hit to kill evil deadpool but then it was too late. The second game we were not even closed to win. Definetely deadpool doesn't know how to write a scheme.
Evil Deadpool is truly evil with the Hyper Insane Healing Factor tactic that I drew 3 times. No tables were flipped because I am going on vacation tomorrow.

First time playing with a veiled scheme, was not expecting it to be that exciting to unveil the scheme! I decided to replay my game when it was time to unveil, because I had 1 escape and was hoping to do better, although that was the exact turn (25) when I would've been able to hit Loki for the last time. I wrote down my "what could've been" scores just to compare them to my second game. In the second game my PPT and escapes were better, but it took me 2 turns longer to win. I hope it was worth the replay!
Also I haven't played with/against cards from the core set in a long time, as I'm always trying to play more with the newest sets. It was nice seeing them again!

My wife said it was not a fun set-up. I picked gambit to pair with Warpath since they both had top deck manipulation, but it didn’t work out for her as her gambit cards didn’t show. I used Hulk and Strong guy to just be Big Mean Green punching machines. That worked well. Rogue was the linchpin since she could easily fit in either deck. The unveiled scheme threw me for a loop but we managed to beat it.

Kept the bank clear and dodged the first three Twists which was a good thing because it came down to the final turn and I didn't have 8 recruit to save the third Fallen Messiah, instead I sent Loki running for the hills.

I luckily drew schemes early and was hanging on two cards in the fallen messiah stack the entire game until I finally beat Loki. That was a stressful one! But fun!

I liked the setup, but it was hard. Game 1 I was able to get my bystander engine going, but I had 3 escapes and hadn't seen Widow's uncommon yet when twist 4 came out. Decide to bail before the unveil and try again. Game 2 I got Widow's uncommon right away, but couldn't get my bystander engine going. I lucked out with 3 cheaper Thor cards, surviving the scheme, but it took too long to get a good amount of attack. I ended up with 3 escapes again this game too (damn you chivalrous duel!). In the end my second hand with Hulk and Strong Guy had to hit the Mastermind 4 times.

column35: This was my first time actually using the sidekicks. I really like the way I can use my extra points to get them even if it's just for one play and then back into the kick pile they go. This was by far the most difficult scheme I have played. Once the reveal happened I knew that I was toast because I didn't buy many good purchase cards and all of the Mutant Messiahs were +3 cost to defeat/ gain and then that quickly turned into +5! It was rough... but super fun., I will for certain be trying this set up again. "M" was really cool once I saw her cards. I was able to get the tactical formation "333" to pop off once and I felt pretty good about that. I am interested to see how other people are able to set the up the more complex tactical formations. Like Strong Guy's "Go Big" is a "445" tactical formation and you only get +3 fight for it. I'd like to know how much of the value of that card is wrapped up in the tactical formation or if people are just pulling this card as a 2 fight Green activation... or are people prioritizing this card at all... I am super happy to be learning about all of the little interactions of this game.  So much fun.

Well, I am going to be petitioning for a change in rules for these schemes. We played a second game "for fun" with the exact same heroes and achieved 2.24 PPT in 5 more turns and won... wish we could have used that one...
Fing fang foom was big and the choose of hitting him or hitting the ring that kept landing in the bank was hard. We found some good combos. Challenging but fun.
Fun game, got to use some sets and heroes I've never had the chance to and FFF is harder than i thought

I took a gamble with my Hero selections: I had no Tech in the Hero Deck at all. I figured the chances of ever having all 5 classes in-hand at once was pretty slim anyway, and sticking to 4 classes gave me a better shot at hitting Class-triggered abilities. And I think it worked out -although one time I actually DID have all 5 classes in hand, thanks to a lucky recruit of Grant Ward.

I went with Photon to add a bunch of blue and a bit of card draw, and I also thought Hyperspeed had a chance to be fairly potent since the vast majority of the Hero cards in the deck had Attack symbols. Backed her up with Ronin for a bit more blue and green, a bit more Hyperspeed and card draw, and his common that can be any Hero class for a bit more control over Size-Changing. In the end, I was pretty happy with my choices. Morbius definitely ended up being the heavy-hitter, but keeping the Hero deck to only four colors made it way easier to trigger his red class abilities.

My first game was actually going great - really good deck synergy and Player 2 had a ton of red and could pretty consistently hit Morbius' Insatiable Craving for 7 attack. But I took a risk of hitting Fin Fang Foom once and leaving a villain in the Blood Bank, and the next two cards were Scheme Twists which ended the game. Argh.

Second game took a little longer to warm up, and I had to do some real dancing one turn to stop a Villain escape, but after that I cleaned out the city and was never in real danger of losing. I had a couple of Man-Thing's card that let me move Villains around to keep the Blood Bank clear. Ended up winning the game with a completely empty city; it just took a long time. I was also a bit lucky; there were only 10 cards left in the Villain deck at the end, and *5* of them were Scheme Twists. I could easily have hit 3 in a row and that would have finished me off.

So, overall, a fun setup!
This was a challenging setup, but was fun. I took way too long.

wanted to try totally awesome hulk and ms marvel in game one to see if champions was a viable strategy with cheering crowd etc, but by insane bad luck i lost to the scheme by twist-chaining very early on... so didnt rly get a feel for a swap of those heroes for a more obvious choice like ronin for this setup,so i ended up using the same team of heroes;
which didn't work out very well.

Going all in on Morbius for one side was super fun, just stacking as much blood frenzy as I could made for some very heavy hitting hands. Unsurpisingly he and Man-Thing worked together as monster bros

first game was rough, had 4 scheme twist chain early (even though we miscounted the strikes and only had 4, which we corrected for the second game). got a Howard the Duck and Man thing deck rolling on game 2, second hand had Morbius and Scarlet Witch, technically had all five colors even though we were very light on tech and blue. game wwent a little long but felt like a string game to end the tourney on. had a great time, thanks for setting all this up!

My first game went so horribly I stopped playing. I knew there was no hope of winning and if I somehow managed it it would’ve been a terrible score. The HQ was expensive and by turn ten I had no heroes in the left hand and three wounds. Just absolutely horrible.

Second game went so much better. The right side really went off with my added heroes of ant man and hulk buster iron man, paired with Mr fantastic it was a great deck that hit modok twice because of his mechanics multiple times. Drew the entire deck two times so long as I didn’t hit an agent. 
Left side was a bit of a hodgepodge, but its main job was getting scarlet witch out of the city so the right didn’t have to clog its deck with her. It was adequate, if bloated because of that. 

In the end it was a really frustrating setup but probably because the first game I suffered from a high cost HQ. And because the second was much friendlier I’m rating the setup highly. Just beware what heroes you choose! Ant-man is expensive it you don't have cheap tech cards!

The setup had satisfying subtleties which added to the fun and the tension. Like the decision to use Cytoplasm Spikes had the potential to K.O. non-grey heroes which got you closer to losing the Scheme Evil Wins condition. I chose She-Hulk and Silver Surfer to lean into Recruit generation with the ability to convert surplus Recruit into Focus abilities. It readily allowed me to fight M.O.D.O.K.'s transformed side as well. The decks came together nicely. Game 1 I got drowned in Wounds by 2 Cosmic Hulk Robots and several Master Strikes. I prioritized cards that could thin them back out, but it felt like bailing water from a sinking ship. A brutal point in Game 1 was Feasting a card from Cytoplasm Spikes, and it hit my Thing rare card before I could ever play it (even though the probability was highly unlikely). I finally managed to stabilize in game 1 and pulled out the win, but I went ahead and shuffled up the same setup for a second attempt. Game 2 went even better! It was off to a very rough start by flipping the Scheme on only turn 8, but the villain deck was more friendly to me after that, my decks hit their stride, and I knocked out M.O.D.O.K even faster! I think I punched him 3 times in one turn! Fun setup with good tension!

I felt pretty confident going into this one.  I thought I had chosen good Heroes and the setup seemed textured but not too hard.  It turned out to be a lot more challenging than I anticipated, but I got quite lucky on my first attempt and managed it in what seemed like pretty good time.  As it turned out, my well-chosen Heroes didn't make a bit of difference-- I didn't play a single one in the attempt that I submitted, and ran the whole thing with just the FF.

The first and second games were totally different experiences. We got off to a fast start in the first game, but the second we got behind (I did... my wife was a star) and got filled with Wounds. Villains escaped left and right.
Interesting challenge for hero choices. Hiroim could have swapped for any other hero as he never made it into our decks in both games.
Thanks
Won first attempt, lost second attempt. Was a fun setup.

The game was fine overall. This was originally counted as a win and was our first attempt. However, we unfortunately realized during our second attempt that, due to a clarification that we had not realized, we had actually lost this game on our last turn, instead of won. During the last turn, we KO'd a hero to hit the Final Blow on the Mastermind and win the game. However we realized during our second game that this KO'd hero actually would have triggered the scheme's "evil wins" condition before winning the game, thus resulting in a loss. Obviously had we realized this during play, we would have just not KO'd the hero and kept the game going for a win. But we realized this in the middle of our second game already (obviously way too late), so we had to count this one as a loss to keep the game fair. However, the fun factor was fine. We got hit by a fair amount of scheme twists very early in the game, disrupting our HQ, clogging it with cards used for only one of the deck, and hindering our deck acceleration. On top of that, we did not hit very many henchmen early game to be able to feast on grey heroes without worry and thinning our decks early. Luck of the draw on that one, but that's how the game plays out sometimes! 

(and yes, our second game was much worse than our first which is why we still submitted the first)
no problem with the game, but I had to play out of town on a very cramped surface, so it wasn't as fun as I normally have with Legendary

I had mixed feelings about this setup. Iron Man + Captain America is a powerful combo. I paired it with Gambit + Cyclops to basically have left and right hands each focus on one pair.  I don't enjoy the schemes that KO heroes in the HQ. I played poorly in Game 1 because of bad timing for scheme twists, I played really well in Game 2 just for better luck. Too much random chance involved. The hero combos let me feel really powerful in Game 2, but the game changes from being too hard to too easy just based on randomness.

Been a long time since I played just straight Core expansion and was even nervous about it, however, I picked Black Widow and Storm mainly for some Bystander rescuing and also in hopes for some weakened attacks on the Roof Tops. I thought about going for Gambit and Rogue, however, I was very pleased with the two I chose to take down the Master of Magnetism.
Fun game but difficult to get enough decent cards before being wiped by scheme twists. Faired much better on first attempt!

I love small Hero Decks though SHCW is always a bit nerve racking. I figured this was my last week since I only have 12 points and even with max points I don't think I can pull up to elimination rounds. I just tried to have fun and this one was great. I do wish I could have had more KO power not even Jug could help me since with both times he showed up I only had a discard pile for one deck total.

Been a while with a Core only game! Tried to bust out the big guns since it’s the final round before cuts. And they didn’t disappoint, even if the scheme forces you to adjust on the fly. Left side was an effective rainbow deck, springing in Rogue’s “Borrowed Brawn” for some big turns. Right side got going a bit later, but a nice Black Widow bystander build that trimmed most of the starters out. Likely needed to be faster, but was better than attempt 2!

Just went with our favorites for this game and both did a great job. Rogue + Cap did their job to get enough attack to take on the Mastermind with one hand, and Iron Man + Fury did the same for the other one, though we never mustered enough Recruit points to get any of the rares.

Left hand went heavy blue and crushed it. Right hand had a lot of struggle early due to low attack, low KO, and poor recruit choices. Felt bad letting an escape happen, but no henchmen came out early.

The hands did not come together fast enough.  All of Black Widow's uncommon fell to the scheme in attempt 1. First attempt I would have had final blow on the next turn.  Second attempt I was super judicious with recruiting and it didn't matter, I even got two rare's from Paibok but couldn't ever get the cards to draw in the right order (even with 13 starter's KO'd).  Was a shame.  I had a lot of fun in the first attempt.

Felt like I was treading water on both attempts...couldn't get any momentum to hit the MM...was still fun, just was a little frustrating, as I was conscious of the turns it was taking...will keep plying my trade and will hopefully get better...
I had 1 wound left in the wound stack (that setup is fun but brutal).

column35: The Legacy Virus is gross lol

I really liked being able to focus on a team of one person getting all the hydra villains.  When you defeated the mastermind each time Dr. Doom had fun card effects.  We really enjoyed having to strategize which villains to attack when and which villians to leave alone.  "Cut off a head of hydra and two more will take its place"....evil turds...  With the wounds situation it was really important to pay attention to that and also not use Hulk without caution.  Nick fury was great for black cards, other than that...kinda lame.  Ironman was a fun addition.  Black cards saved us the first time, but the synergy of blue storm and ironman really made the team start kicking. We got very few cyclops cards.  We did not draw one of his green determination cards either game (extra recruit when discard a card) which blew my mind.  One person was successful and the other struggled on both games.  Kind of interesting how that worked.

We had no issues having trench cards for twist and strikes so the wound stack was not close to empty. We could just never draw enough strikes to hit Doom. Several turns where we had 8, one strike away.
The games went to fast, wish there were more wounds
